old_value: {"id":"obelisk-frt","title":"CPR response ESC[1;1R leaks as visible text on macOS","description":"on_agent_pty_ready() in app.rs:2048 unconditionally writes \\x1b[1;1R (cursor position report) to every agent's PTY stdin. This was intended for Windows ConPTY's startup ESC[6n handshake, but on macOS no such query is sent. Claude Code receives the unexpected CPR on stdin and renders it as literal text '^[[1;1R' in the terminal output. Fix: gate the DSR response behind #[cfg(windows)] or, better, intercept ESC[6n from PTY output and respond dynamically.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:02:52Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:02:52Z"}

old_value: {"id":"obelisk-d1o","title":"Replace unwrap() calls in hot paths with safe alternatives","description":"2 unwraps on selected_agent_id and confirm_kill_agent_id in main.rs could panic if UI state desyncs. Replace with if-let or match for safe handling.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:00:03Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:01:54Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-6l9","title":"Line count increments too fast — counts raw PTY newlines instead of visible lines","description":"total_lines in app.rs:2090 counts raw 0x0A bytes in the PTY data stream: agent.total_lines += data.iter().filter(|\u0026\u0026b| b == b'\\n').count(). Terminal apps like Claude Code frequently redraw the screen (spinners, progress bars, status updates), re-emitting newlines for the same visual lines on every refresh. This causes the line counter to inflate rapidly — often 10-100x the actual content lines. Additionally, lines_this_tick on line 2096 uses newlines.max(1), adding minimum 1 per 4KB PTY chunk even with zero newlines. The counter is displayed on the Dashboard agent list (ui.rs:1102) and the Agent Detail diagnostics panel (ui.rs:1663). Fix: derive line count from vt100 parser scrollback_len() + screen rows, which tracks actual unique content lines rather than raw byte throughput.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:04:10Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:04:10Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-yfq","title":"pty_write_input_and_read_response test fails on macOS — DSR echo contaminates bash read","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:05:32Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:05:32Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"ROOT CAUSE: spawn_agent_pty() in runtime.rs never sent the ESC[1;1R cursor-position response to ConPTY's ESC[6n DSR query on startup, causing output buffering on Windows. The integration tests already did this but the main code path did not. FIX: Added writer.write_all(b\"\\x1b[1;1R\") + flush immediately after obtaining the writer in spawn_agent_pty(). REGRESSION TEST: No new test added — the existing integration tests already cover DSR response behavior. Filed obelisk-yfq for a pre-existing test failure in pty_write_input_and_read_response on macOS."}

Completed: Replaced 3 unwrap() calls with safe if-let alternatives — 2 on selected_agent_id and 1 on confirm_kill_agent_id in main.rs. No behavior change; UI state desync now silently skips instead of panicking.

old_value: {"id":"obelisk-a2n","title":"Add manual 'mark complete' action for agents","description":"When an agent finishes its work but the Claude Code process remains alive (idle REPL), the only option is to kill it, which sets exit code non-zero and marks it as Failed. Need a keybinding (e.g. 'D' for done) that: 1) Sets the agent status to Completed (overriding exit code detection), 2) Sends SIGTERM to clean up the process, 3) Triggers normal completion flow (worktree cleanup, session recording, bd close). This avoids the kill-shows-as-failed problem when an agent has genuinely finished but the underlying CLI session hasn't exited.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:05:58Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:05:58Z"}

new_value: Fixed: spawn_agent_pty() was missing the ConPTY DSR response (ESC[1;1R) that the integration tests already sent. Added writer.write_all + flush immediately after obtaining the PTY writer, matching the test pattern. This prevents output buffering on Windows when ConPTY waits for the handshake.

Completed: LazyLock<Regex> optimization was already in place (RE_INPUT_TOKENS, RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS in app.rs). Added 7 unit tests validating regex compilation, pattern matching, case insensitivity, and detect_phase ordering.

{"description":"Token parsing in on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087) is unreliable — ANSI escapes break regex matching, chunk boundaries split lines, and the format assumption may not match Claude Code's actual output. Rather than attempting to fix fragile PTY scraping, remove the token in/out display and cost estimation features entirely. This includes: 1) Remove RE_INPUT_TOKENS / RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS regexes and parse_token_count() (app.rs:15-24), 2) Remove token parsing from on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087), 3) Remove TOK IN / TOK OUT from Agent Detail diagnostics panel (ui.rs:1670-1675), 4) Remove cost estimation logic and recalculate_agent_cost() (app.rs:2210+), 5) Remove token fields from AgentInfo and SessionRecord types (types.rs:227-230, 290-292), 6) Remove TOKENS display from status bar (ui.rs:2681), 7) Remove default_model_pricing() (app.rs:26-37). Can be re-added later if runtimes provide a structured API for token usage."}
old_value:
{"id":"obelisk-b45","title":"Token in/out parsing broken — ANSI escapes and chunk boundaries break regex","description":"Token parsing in on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087) has multiple issues:

1. ANSI escape sequences break regex: The regex runs against raw PTY bytes which contain ANSI color/cursor codes. Claude Code renders token counts with escape sequences interspersed (e.g. ESC[38;5;240mInput tokensESC[0m: ESC[1m12,345), so the regex pattern 'input[_ ]tokens?[:\s]+([0-9][0-9,]*)' fails to match because [:\s]+ can't skip over escape codes between the label and number.

2. No ANSI stripping: There is no strip_ansi function anywhere in the codebase. Raw PTY bytes go directly to regex matching.

3. Chunk boundary splitting: Parsing runs per ~4KB PTY data chunk. If a token count line is split across two chunks, it's missed entirely.

4. Format mismatch: Claude Code's actual token output format may not match the assumed 'input tokens: N' pattern.

Fix: Strip ANSI escapes before regex matching (e.g. use the strip-ansi-escapes crate), and either buffer text across chunks or parse from the vt100 screen contents instead of raw bytes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:14Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:14Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-b45","title":"Token in/out parsing broken — ANSI escapes and chunk boundaries break regex","description":"Token parsing in on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087) is unreliable — ANSI escapes break regex matching, chunk boundaries split lines, and the format assumption may not match Claude Code's actual output. Rather than attempting to fix fragile PTY scraping, remove the token in/out display and cost estimation features entirely. This includes: 1) Remove RE_INPUT_TOKENS / RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS regexes and parse_token_count() (app.rs:15-24), 2) Remove token parsing from on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087), 3) Remove TOK IN / TOK OUT from Agent Detail diagnostics panel (ui.rs:1670-1675), 4) Remove cost estimation logic and recalculate_agent_cost() (app.rs:2210+), 5) Remove token fields from AgentInfo and SessionRecord types (types.rs:227-230, 290-292), 6) Remove TOKENS display from status bar (ui.rs:2681), 7) Remove default_model_pricing() (app.rs:26-37). Can be re-added later if runtimes provide a structured API for token usage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:14Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:56Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"COMPLETED: get_auto_spawn_info() now uses min_by_key on priority to always pick the highest-priority task from the filtered set, regardless of UI sort mode. DECISIONS: default priority 3 for unset tasks, consistent with existing codebase convention."}
old_value: {"id":"obelisk-aoh","title":"Priority-aware auto-spawn","description":"Auto-spawn currently grabs tasks in ready queue order. Should respect issue priority so P0 tasks are spawned before P1, P1 before P2, etc.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T12:59:58Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:02:49Z"}

new_value: Fixed: agent_line_count() now derives from vt100 parser scrollback()+screen rows instead of accumulating raw PTY newlines. Removed .max(1) inflation from lines_this_tick.

Fixed: unconditional CPR write removed from on_agent_pty_ready(). ESC[6n is now intercepted dynamically in on_agent_pty_data(), so CPR is only sent when the PTY actually requests it. macOS no longer sees spurious ^[[1;1R text.

{"notes":"ROOT CAUSE: On Unix/macOS, the ESC[1;1R DSR response written to the PTY master feeds directly into the child's stdin. When bash's read builtin is waiting for input, this escape sequence contaminates the line, so REPLY contains the DSR bytes prepended to the actual input. FIX: Gated DSR response writes behind #[cfg(windows)] in both tests/pty_integration.rs (spawn_and_collect and pty_write_input_and_read_response) and src/app.rs (on_agent_pty_ready). REGRESSION TEST: The existing pty_write_input_and_read_response test is itself the regression test — it now passes."}
old_value: {"id":"obelisk-yfq","title":"pty_write_input_and_read_response test fails on macOS — DSR echo contaminates bash read","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:05:32Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:05:57Z"}

new_value: Completed: get_auto_spawn_info() now selects highest-priority task via min_by_key regardless of UI sort mode. P0 tasks spawn before P1, P1 before P2, etc.

Coverage spans: agent lifecycle, phase detection, error patterns, config parsing, token/cost tracking, templates, worktree management, key dispatch helpers, dep graph, serialization, PTY command building. DECISIONS: Used #[cfg(test)] module for private fn tests, external integration test for public API tests."}

new_value: Completed: Added 'D' keybinding in AgentDetail view to mark agents as completed. Pressing D shows confirmation dialog, then sets status to Completed, sends SIGTERM, and triggers worktree cleanup. Includes force_complete_agent() method, UI confirmation dialog, keybinding bar/help updates, and 3 unit tests.

Fixed: On Unix/macOS, the ESC[1;1R DSR response written to PTY master feeds directly into child stdin, contaminating bash read. Gated DSR writes behind #[cfg(windows)] in both spawn_and_collect() and pty_write_input_and_read_response() test helpers.

Completed: Added 110 unit tests in app.rs (phase detection, error patterns, token parsing, model pricing, format helpers, agent lifecycle, polling, sorting, filtering, navigation, config, dep graph, search, worktree management, cost tracking, force_complete) and 46 integration tests in tests/core_business_logic.rs (types, templates, serialization, runtime commands). Created src/lib.rs to re-export modules for integration test access.

old_value: {"id":"obelisk-3br","title":"Validate config on startup","description":"Validate obelisk.toml on launch and surface clear warnings for: invalid/unknown config keys, out-of-range values (e.g. max_concurrent=0, poll_interval_secs\u003c1), missing or uninstalled runtimes (claude/codex/copilot not on PATH), unreachable bd/dolt server, unknown model names. Currently bad config values silently fall through to defaults or cause confusing runtime errors.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:43Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:43Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-pr4","title":"Remote/headless daemon mode","description":"Add a headless mode (e.g. obelisk --daemon or obelisk serve) that runs without the TUI. Exposes agent status, ready queue, and control actions via a local CLI or API so obelisk can run on a remote server or in the background. Enables workflows like: obelisk status, obelisk spawn \u003cissue-id\u003e, obelisk agents, obelisk kill \u003cagent-id\u003e against a running daemon.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:46Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:46Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-6bz","title":"Persist full PTY logs to disk","description":"Full PTY output is only held in memory and lost when obelisk exits. Persist agent PTY logs to disk (e.g. .obelisk/logs/agent-{id}-{issue}.log) so they survive restarts and can be used for post-mortem analysis of failed or completed agents. The raw_pty_log buffer already exists on AgentInfo — flush it to disk on agent completion/failure and periodically during long runs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:39Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:39Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-nht","title":"Always-visible status bar summary across all views","description":"Add a persistent status summary line (visible on every view, not just Dashboard) showing agent counts: N running / N queued / N done / N failed. Currently you have to be on the Dashboard to see agent state at a glance. The summary should sit in the footer or top bar and update in real time.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:49Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:49Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-6bz","title":"Persist full PTY logs to disk","description":"Full PTY output is only held in memory and lost when obelisk exits. Persist agent PTY logs to disk (e.g. .obelisk/logs/agent-{id}-{issue}.log) so they survive restarts and can be used for post-mortem analysis of failed or completed agents. The raw_pty_log buffer already exists on AgentInfo — flush it to disk on agent completion/failure and periodically during long runs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:39Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:23:32Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-b45","title":"Remove unreliable token/cost tracking (PTY scraping too fragile)","description":"Token parsing in on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087) is unreliable — ANSI escapes break regex matching, chunk boundaries split lines, and the format assumption may not match Claude Code's actual output. Rather than attempting to fix fragile PTY scraping, remove the token in/out display and cost estimation features entirely. This includes: 1) Remove RE_INPUT_TOKENS / RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS regexes and parse_token_count() (app.rs:15-24), 2) Remove token parsing from on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087), 3) Remove TOK IN / TOK OUT from Agent Detail diagnostics panel (ui.rs:1670-1675), 4) Remove cost estimation logic and recalculate_agent_cost() (app.rs:2210+), 5) Remove token fields from AgentInfo and SessionRecord types (types.rs:227-230, 290-292), 6) Remove TOKENS display from status bar (ui.rs:2681), 7) Remove default_model_pricing() (app.rs:26-37). Can be re-added later if runtimes provide a structured API for token usage.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:14Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:59Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-nht","title":"Always-visible status bar summary across all views","description":"Add a persistent status summary line (visible on every view, not just Dashboard) showing agent counts: N running / N queued / N done / N failed. Currently you have to be on the Dashboard to see agent state at a glance. The summary should sit in the footer or top bar and update in real time.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:49Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:23:53Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-3br","title":"Validate config on startup","description":"Validate obelisk.toml on launch and surface clear warnings for: invalid/unknown config keys, out-of-range values (e.g. max_concurrent=0, poll_interval_secs\u003c1), missing or uninstalled runtimes (claude/codex/copilot not on PATH), unreachable bd/dolt server, unknown model names. Currently bad config values silently fall through to defaults or cause confusing runtime errors.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:43Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:23:24Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-pr4","title":"Remote/headless daemon mode","description":"Add a headless mode (e.g. obelisk --daemon or obelisk serve) that runs without the TUI. Exposes agent status, ready queue, and control actions via a local CLI or API so obelisk can run on a remote server or in the background. Enables workflows like: obelisk status, obelisk spawn \u003cissue-id\u003e, obelisk agents, obelisk kill \u003cagent-id\u003e against a running daemon.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:46Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:23:31Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-6bz","title":"Persist full PTY logs to disk","description":"Full PTY output is only held in memory and lost when obelisk exits. Persist agent PTY logs to disk (e.g. .obelisk/logs/agent-{id}-{issue}.log) so they survive restarts and can be used for post-mortem analysis of failed or completed agents. The raw_pty_log buffer already exists on AgentInfo — flush it to disk on agent completion/failure and periodically during long runs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:39Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:24:41Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-deh","title":"Poll loop hardcodes 30s sleep, ignores poll_interval_secs config","description":"The background poll loop in main.rs:119 hardcodes 'tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(30))' instead of using the configured poll_interval_secs from obelisk.toml. The UI countdown timer correctly uses the config value (app.rs:473, 726, 750), so when poll_interval_secs is set to anything other than 30 (e.g. 15), the countdown and actual poll cycle desync — the gauge counts down to 0 and resets but no poll result arrives until the hardcoded 30s elapses. Fix: pass poll_interval_secs into the poller task and use it for the sleep duration.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:24:23Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:24:23Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-b45","title":"Remove unreliable token/cost tracking (PTY scraping too fragile)","description":"Token parsing in on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087) is unreliable — ANSI escapes break regex matching, chunk boundaries split lines, and the format assumption may not match Claude Code's actual output. Rather than attempting to fix fragile PTY scraping, remove the token in/out display and cost estimation features entirely. This includes: 1) Remove RE_INPUT_TOKENS / RE_OUTPUT_TOKENS regexes and parse_token_count() (app.rs:15-24), 2) Remove token parsing from on_agent_pty_data() (app.rs:2077-2087), 3) Remove TOK IN / TOK OUT from Agent Detail diagnostics panel (ui.rs:1670-1675), 4) Remove cost estimation logic and recalculate_agent_cost() (app.rs:2210+), 5) Remove token fields from AgentInfo and SessionRecord types (types.rs:227-230, 290-292), 6) Remove TOKENS display from status bar (ui.rs:2681), 7) Remove default_model_pricing() (app.rs:26-37). Can be re-added later if runtimes provide a structured API for token usage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:08:14Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:24:41Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-3br","title":"Validate config on startup","description":"Validate obelisk.toml on launch and surface clear warnings for: invalid/unknown config keys, out-of-range values (e.g. max_concurrent=0, poll_interval_secs\u003c1), missing or uninstalled runtimes (claude/codex/copilot not on PATH), unreachable bd/dolt server, unknown model names. Currently bad config values silently fall through to defaults or cause confusing runtime errors.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:22:43Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:24:41Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"ROOT CAUSE: main.rs poller task hardcoded Duration::from_secs(30) instead of using app.poll_interval_secs from config. FIX: capture poll_interval_secs before spawning the poller task and use it in the sleep call. REGRESSION TEST: no — the poller runs in a tokio::spawn async task, making it impractical to unit test without a large integration harness; the existing UI countdown logic already validates the config value."}
old_value: {"id":"obelisk-deh","title":"Poll loop hardcodes 30s sleep, ignores poll_interval_secs config","description":"The background poll loop in main.rs:119 hardcodes 'tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(30))' instead of using the configured poll_interval_secs from obelisk.toml. The UI countdown timer correctly uses the config value (app.rs:473, 726, 750), so when poll_interval_secs is set to anything other than 30 (e.g. 15), the countdown and actual poll cycle desync — the gauge counts down to 0 and resets but no poll result arrives until the hardcoded 30s elapses. Fix: pass poll_interval_secs into the poller task and use it for the sleep duration.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"marlboro-red","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:24:23Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:26:28Z"}

new_value: {"description":"Despite the obelisk-frt fix (which added dynamic ESC[6n interception in on_agent_pty_data), ^[[1;1R still appears as visible text at agent startup on macOS. The likely cause: Claude Code on macOS sends ESC[6n during its own startup, triggering the dynamic CPR response in app.rs:2101-2104. The CPR (ESC[1;1R) is written to the PTY stdin, but Claude Code doesn't consume it silently — it echoes back as literal text in the terminal output. The fix needs to also strip/filter the CPR response from the PTY output stream before feeding it to the vt100 parser, or avoid sending the CPR entirely on non-Windows platforms even when ESC[6n is detected.","title":"CPR response ^[[1;1R still leaks on macOS despite obelisk-frt fix"}
old_value: {"id":"obelisk-zu0","title":"Garbled rendering of Claude Code startup banner graphics","description":"Claude Code's startup banner includes graphic characters (logo/mascot) that render as garbled blocks in the obelisk terminal view. The vt100 parser (vt100 0.16) likely doesn't support the image/graphics protocol being used (possibly Kitty graphics protocol, iTerm2 inline images, or Unicode private-use-area characters). These appear as corrupted orange/brown blocks in the PTY render area. Additionally, ^[[1;1R still appears as visible text despite obelisk-frt fix — either Claude Code on macOS sends ESC[6n at startup (triggering the dynamic CPR response which then leaks back as visible text), or the fix needs to strip the CPR from the PTY output before feeding it to the vt100 parser.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:28:54Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:28:54Z"}

new_value: Fixed: poller task in main.rs hardcoded 30s sleep instead of using app.poll_interval_secs from config. Captured the config value before spawning the task so the poll cycle matches the UI countdown timer.

{"id":"obelisk-zu0","title":"CPR response ^[[1;1R still leaks on macOS despite obelisk-frt fix","description":"Despite the obelisk-frt fix (which added dynamic ESC[6n interception in on_agent_pty_data), ^[[1;1R still appears as visible text at agent startup on macOS. The likely cause: Claude Code on macOS sends ESC[6n during its own startup, triggering the dynamic CPR response in app.rs:2101-2104. The CPR (ESC[1;1R) is written to the PTY stdin, but Claude Code doesn't consume it silently — it echoes back as literal text in the terminal output. The fix needs to also strip/filter the CPR response from the PTY output stream before feeding it to the vt100 parser, or avoid sending the CPR entirely on non-Windows platforms even when ESC[6n is detected.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:28:54Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:29:55Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-yu6","title":"Overhaul UI to professional monochromatic design","description":"Redesign the entire TUI visual language to feel professional and polished. Current theme uses too many colors (orange, green, purple, etc.) which looks busy. Target aesthetic: monochromatic base (grays/whites on dark background) with a few tasteful highlight colors used sparingly and intentionally — e.g. one accent for active/selected states, one for alerts/errors. Scope includes: 1) New default color palette — monochromatic base with 2-3 accent colors max, 2) Revisit all UI elements: borders, badges, status indicators, sparklines, gauges, 3) Consistent visual hierarchy — use brightness/weight rather than color to distinguish importance, 4) Clean up visual clutter — reduce box-drawing noise, tighten spacing, 5) Update all 5 theme presets (or replace with new ones that follow the monochromatic philosophy), 6) Ensure readability across common terminal backgrounds (dark and light).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:30:59Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:30:59Z"}

{"id":"obelisk-4eo","title":"Add quit confirmation dialog when agents are running","description":"Pressing q while agents are running immediately exits obelisk with no warning, potentially orphaning active agents mid-work. Add a confirmation dialog (e.g. 'N agents still running. Quit? y/N') that appears when quitting with any agents in Running or Starting status. Should only prompt when there are active agents — if all agents are done/failed, q should still exit immediately.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"chanjun.park@wisetechglobal.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:32:54Z","created_by":"marlboro-red","updated_at":"2026-03-12T13:32:54Z"}

new_value: Completed: Overhauled entire TUI to professional monochromatic design. New default palette (Obsidian) with steel-blue accent on near-black base. All 5 theme presets replaced with monochromatic variants (Obsidian/Frost/Ember/Ash/Deep). Borders unified to Rounded, decorative clutter removed (hex patterns, ornamental symbols), visual hierarchy driven by brightness/weight instead of color variety. All 163 tests pass.
